briefdescription.para: Flags for XPath engine compilation and runtime.
forbid variables in expression
The set of XPath error codes.
An expression is evaluated to yield an object, which has one of the following four basic types:
XPath context.
XPath parser and evaluation context.
XPath node set.
XPath object.
An XPath function.

detaileddescription.para: The arguments (if any) are popped out from the context stack and the result is pushed on the stack.

memberdef.definition: typedef xmlXPathObject *(* xmlXPathVariableLookupFunc) (void *ctxt, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *ns_uri)
memberdef.name: xmlXPathVariableLookupFunc

briefdescription.para: Prototype for callbacks used to plug variable lookup in the XPath engine.

parameterdescription.para: the namespace name hosting this variable

simplesect.para: the XPath object value or NULL if not found.

memberdef.definition: typedef xmlXPathFunction(* xmlXPathFuncLookupFunc) (void *ctxt, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *ns_uri)
memberdef.name: xmlXPathFuncLookupFunc

briefdescription.para: Prototype for callbacks used to plug function lookup in the XPath engine.

parameterdescription.para: the namespace name hosting this function

simplesect.para: the XPath function or NULL if not found.

briefdescription.para: Compiled XPath expression.
Objects and Nodesets handling.
Create a new xmlNodeSet of type double and of value val

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: Free up the xmlXPathObject obj but don't deallocate the objects in the list contrary to xmlXPathFreeObject.
Free the NodeSet compound (not the actual nodes !).
allocate a new copy of a given object

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: Compare two nodes w.r.t document order.

simplesect.para: -2 in case of error 1 if first point < second point, 0 if it's the same node, -1 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Conversion functions to basic types.

simplesect.para: the boolean value

briefdescription.para: Converts a string to its boolean value.

simplesect.para: the boolean value

briefdescription.para: Converts a node-set to its boolean value.

simplesect.para: the boolean value

briefdescription.para: Converts an XPath object to its boolean value.

simplesect.para: the boolean value

briefdescription.para: Converts a boolean to its number value.

simplesect.para: the number value

briefdescription.para: Converts a string to its number value.

simplesect.para: the number value

briefdescription.para: Converts a node to its number value.

simplesect.para: the number value

briefdescription.para: Converts a node-set to its number value.

simplesect.para: the number value

briefdescription.para: Converts an XPath object to its number value.

simplesect.para: the number value

briefdescription.para: Converts a boolean to its string value.

simplesect.para: a newly allocated string.

briefdescription.para: Converts a number to its string value.

simplesect.para: a newly allocated string.

briefdescription.para: Converts a node to its string value.

simplesect.para: a newly allocated string.

briefdescription.para: Converts a node-set to its string value.

simplesect.para: a newly allocated string.

briefdescription.para: Converts an existing object to its string() equivalent.

simplesect.para: the allocated string value of the object, NULL in case of error. It's up to the caller to free the string memory with xmlFree.

briefdescription.para: Converts an existing object to its boolean() equivalent.

simplesect.para: the new object, the old one is freed (or the operation is done directly on val)

briefdescription.para: Converts an existing object to its number() equivalent.

simplesect.para: the new object, the old one is freed (or the operation is done directly on val)

briefdescription.para: Converts an existing object to its string() equivalent.

simplesect.para: the new object, the old one is freed (or the operation is done directly on val)

briefdescription.para: Context handling.

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathContext just allocated. The caller will need to free it.

briefdescription.para: Free up an xmlXPathContext.
Register a callback function that will be called on errors and warnings.

detaileddescription.para: If handler is NULL, the error handler will be deactivated.

parameterdescription.para: user data which will be passed to the handler

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlXPathContext *ctxt, int active, int value, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlXPathContextSetCache

briefdescription.para: Creates/frees an object cache on the XPath context.

detaileddescription.para: If activates XPath objects (xmlXPathObject) will be cached internally to be reused.
options must be set to 0 to enable XPath object caching. Other values for options have currently no effect.
value sets the maximum number of XPath objects to be cached per slot. There are two slots for node-set and misc objects. Use <0 for the default number (100).

parameterdescription.para: a value with semantics dependent on options
options (currently only the value 0 is used)

simplesect.para: 0 if the setting succeeded, and -1 on API or internal errors.

briefdescription.para: Evaluation functions.

detaileddescription.para: This stamps all the element nodes with the document order Like for line information, the order is kept in the element->content field, the value stored is actually - the node number (starting at -1) to be able to differentiate from line numbers.

simplesect.para: the number of elements found in the document or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Sets 'node' as the context node.

detaileddescription.para: The node must be in the same document as that associated with the context.

parameterdescription.para: the node to to use as the context node

simplesect.para: -1 in case of error or 0 if successful

briefdescription.para: Evaluate the XPath Location Path in the given context.

detaileddescription.para: The node 'node' is set as the context node. The context node is not restored.

parameterdescription.para: the node to to use as the context node

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathObject resulting from the evaluation or NULL. the caller has to free the object.

briefdescription.para: Evaluate the XPath Location Path in the given context.

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathObject resulting from the evaluation or NULL. the caller has to free the object.

briefdescription.para: Alias for xmlXPathEval.

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathObject resulting from the evaluation or NULL. the caller has to free the object.

briefdescription.para: Evaluate a predicate result for the current node.

detaileddescription.para: A PredicateExpr is evaluated by evaluating the Expr and converting the result to a boolean. If the result is a number, the result will be converted to true if the number is equal to the position of the context node in the context node list (as returned by the position function) and will be converted to false otherwise; if the result is not a number, then the result will be converted as if by a call to the boolean function.

simplesect.para: 1 if predicate is true, 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Separate compilation/evaluation entry points.

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathCompExpr resulting from the compilation or NULL. the caller has to free the object.

briefdescription.para: Compile an XPath expression.

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathCompExpr resulting from the compilation or NULL. the caller has to free the object.

briefdescription.para: Evaluate the Precompiled XPath expression in the given context.

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathObject resulting from the evaluation or NULL. the caller has to free the object.

briefdescription.para: Applies the XPath boolean() function on the result of the given compiled expression.

simplesect.para: 1 if the expression evaluated to true, 0 if to false and -1 in API and internal errors.

briefdescription.para: Free up the memory allocated by comp
Checks whether a double is a NaN.

simplesect.para: 1 if the value is a NaN, 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Checks whether a double is an infinity.

simplesect.para: 1 if the value is +Infinite, -1 if -Infinite, 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: XML Path Language implementation.

detaileddescription.para: API for the XML Path Language implementation
XML Path Language implementation XPath is a language for addressing parts of an XML document, designed to be used by both XSLT and XPointer http://www.w3.org/TR/xpath

simplesect.para: See Copyright for the status of this software.
Daniel Veillard
